children.heading: Requirement to meet minimum funding standard
children.label: a Requirement to meet minimum funding standard

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A plan to which this section applies shall satisfy the minimum funding standard applicable to the plan for any plan year.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a defined benefit plan which is not a multiemployer plan or a CSEC plan, the employer makes contributions to or under the plan for the plan year which, in the aggregate, are not less than the minimum required contribution determined under section 430 for the plan for the plan year,

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a money purchase plan which is not a multiemployer plan, the employer makes contributions to or under the plan for the plan year which are required under the terms of the plan,

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of a multiemployer plan, the employers make contributions to or under the plan for any plan year which, in the aggregate, are sufficient to ensure that the plan does not have an accumulated funding deficiency under section 431 as of the end of the plan year, and

children.label: D
children.text: in the case of a CSEC plan, the employers make contributions to or under the plan for any plan year which, in the aggregate, are sufficient to ensure that the plan does not have an accumulated funding deficiency under section 433 as of the end of the plan year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the amount of any contribution required by this section (including any required installments under paragraphs (3) and (4) of section 430(j) or under section 433(f)) shall be paid by the employer responsible for making contributions to or under the plan.

children.heading: Joint and several liability where employer member of controlled group
children.label: 2 Joint and several liability where employer member of controlled group
children.text: If the employer referred to in paragraph (1) is a member of a controlled group, each member of such group shall be jointly and severally liable for payment of such contributions.

children.heading: Multiemployer plans in critical status
children.label: 3 Multiemployer plans in critical status
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply in the case of a multiemployer plan for any plan year in which the plan is in critical status pursuant to section 432. This paragraph shall only apply if the plan sponsor adopts a rehabilitation plan in accordance with section 432(e) and complies with such rehabilitation plan (and any modifications of the plan).

children.heading: Variance from minimum funding standards
children.label: c Variance from minimum funding standards

children.heading: Waiver in case of business hardship
children.label: 1 Waiver in case of business hardship
i
children.text: an employer is (or in the case of a multiemployer plan or a CSEC plan, 10 percent or more of the number of employers contributing to or under the plan are) unable to satisfy the minimum funding standard for a plan year without temporary substantial business hardship (substantial business hardship in the case of a multiemployer plan), and

children.label: ii
children.text: application of the standard would be adverse to the interests of plan participants in the aggregate,

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a defined benefit plan which is not a multiemployer plan or a CSEC plan, the minimum required contribution under section 430 for the plan year shall be reduced by the amount of the waived funding deficiency and such amount shall be amortized as required under section 430(e),

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a multiemployer plan, the funding standard account shall be credited under section 431(b)(3)(C) with the amount of the waived funding deficiency and such amount shall be amortized as required under section 431(b)(2)(C), and

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of a CSEC plan, the funding standard account shall be credited under section 433(b)(3)(C) with the amount of the waived funding deficiency and such amount shall be amortized as required under section 433(b)(2)(C).

children.heading: Waiver of amortized portion not allowed
children.label: C Waiver of amortized portion not allowed
children.text: The Secretary may not waive under subparagraph (A) any portion of the minimum funding standard under subsection (a) for a plan year which is attributable to any waived funding deficiency for any preceding plan year.

children.label: A
children.text: the employer is operating at an economic loss,

children.label: B
children.text: there is substantial unemployment or underemployment in the trade or business and in the industry concerned,

children.label: C
children.text: the sales and profits of the industry concerned are depressed or declining, and

children.label: D
children.text: it is reasonable to expect that the plan will be continued only if the waiver is granted.

children.heading: Waived funding deficiency
children.label: 3 Waived funding deficiency
children.text: For purposes of this section and part III of this subchapter, the term “waived funding deficiency” means the portion of the minimum funding standard under subsection (a) (determined without regard to the waiver) for a plan year waived by the Secretary and not satisfied by employer contributions.

children.heading: Security for waivers for single-employer plans, consultations
children.label: 4 Security for waivers for single-employer plans, consultations

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (C), the Secretary may require an employer maintaining a defined benefit plan which is a single-employer plan (within the meaning of section 4001(a)(15) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974) to provide security to such plan as a condition for granting or modifying a waiver under paragraph (1) or for granting an extension under section 433(d).

children.heading: Special rules
children.label: ii Special rules
children.text: Any security provided under clause (i) may be perfected and enforced only by the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation, or at the direction of the Corporation, by a contributing sponsor (within the meaning of section 4001(a)(13) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974), or a member of such sponsor’s controlled group (within the meaning of section 4001(a)(14) of such Act).

children.heading: Consultation with the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation
children.label: B Consultation with the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation
I
children.text: notice of the completed application for any waiver, modification, or extension, and

children.label: II
children.text: an opportunity to comment on such application within 30 days after receipt of such notice, and

children.label: I
children.text: any comments of the Corporation under clause (i)(II), and

children.label: II
children.text: any views of any employee organization (within the meaning of section 3(4) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974) representing participants in the plan which are submitted in writing to the Secretary in connection with such application.

children.heading: Exception for certain waivers or extensions
children.label: C Exception for certain waivers or extensions
I
children.text: the aggregate unpaid minimum required contributions (within the meaning of section 4971(c)(4)) for the plan year and all preceding plan years, or the accumulated funding deficiency under section 433, whichever is applicable,

children.label: II
children.text: the present value of all waiver amortization installments determined for the plan year and succeeding plan years under section 430(e)(2) or 433(b)(2)(C), whichever is applicable, and

children.label: III
children.text: the total amounts not paid by reason of an extension in effect under section 433(d),

children.heading: Treatment of waivers or extensions for which applications are pending
children.label: ii Treatment of waivers or extensions for which applications are pending
children.text: The amount described in clause (i)(I) shall include any increase in such amount which would result if all applications for waivers or extensions with respect to the minimum funding standard under this subsection which are pending with respect to such plan were denied.

children.heading: Application must be submitted before date 2½ months after close of year
children.label: A Application must be submitted before date 2½ months after close of year
children.text: In the case of a defined benefit plan which is not a multiemployer plan, no waiver may be granted under this subsection with respect to any plan for any plan year unless an application therefor is submitted to the Secretary not later than the 15th day of the 3rd month beginning after the close of such plan year.

children.heading: Special rule if employer is member of controlled group
children.label: B Special rule if employer is member of controlled group
i
children.text: with respect to such employer, and

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to the controlled group of which such employer is a member (determined by treating all members of such group as a single employer).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall, before granting a waiver under this subsection, require each applicant to provide evidence satisfactory to the Secretary that the applicant has provided notice of the filing of the application for such waiver to each affected party (as defined in section 4001(a)(21) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974). Such notice shall include a description of the extent to which the plan is funded for benefits which are guaranteed under title IV of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 and for benefit liabilities.

children.heading: Consideration of relevant information
children.label: B Consideration of relevant information
children.text: The Secretary shall consider any relevant information provided by a person to whom notice was given under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No amendment of a plan which increases the liabilities of the plan by reason of any increase in benefits, any change in the accrual of benefits, or any change in the rate at which benefits become nonforfeitable under the plan shall be adopted if a waiver under this subsection or an extension of time under section 431(d) or section 433(d) is in effect with respect to the plan, or if a plan amendment described in subsection (d)(2) which reduces the accrued benefit of any participant has been made at any time in the preceding 12 months (24 months in the case of a multiemployer plan). If a plan is amended in violation of the preceding sentence, any such waiver, or extension of time, shall not apply to any plan year ending on or after the date on which such amendment is adopted.

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretary determines to be reasonable and which provides for only de minimis increases in the liabilities of the plan,

children.label: ii
children.text: only repeals an amendment described in subsection (d)(2), or

children.label: iii
children.text: is required as a condition of qualification under part I of subchapter D of chapter 1.

children.heading: Change in method or year
children.label: 1 Change in method or year
children.text: If the funding method or a plan year for a plan is changed, the change shall take effect only if approved by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: is adopted after the close of such plan year but no later than 2½ months after the close of the plan year (or, in the case of a multiemployer plan, no later than 2 years after the close of such plan year),

children.label: B
children.text: does not reduce the accrued benefit of any participant determined as of the beginning of the first plan year to which the amendment applies, and

children.label: C
children.text: does not reduce the accrued benefit of any participant determined as of the time of adoption except to the extent required by the circumstances,

children.heading: Controlled group
children.label: 3 Controlled group
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “controlled group” means any group treated as a single employer under subsection (b), (c), (m), or () of section 414.o

children.heading: Plans to which section applies
children.label: e Plans to which section applies
A
children.text: such plan included a trust which qualified (or was determined by the Secretary to have qualified) under section 401(a), or

children.label: B
children.text: such plan satisfied (or was determined by the Secretary to have satisfied) the requirements of section 403(a).

children.label: A
children.text: any profit-sharing or stock bonus plan,

children.label: B
children.text: any insurance contract plan described in paragraph (3),

children.label: C
children.text: any governmental plan (within the meaning of section 414(d)),

children.label: D
children.text: any church plan (within the meaning of section 414(e)) with respect to which the election provided by section 410(d) has not been made,

children.label: E
children.text: any plan which has not, at any time after , provided for employer contributions, orSeptember 2, 1974

children.label: F
children.text: any plan established and maintained by a society, order, or association described in section 501(c)(8) or (9), if no part of the contributions to or under such plan are made by employers of participants in such plan.

children.label: A
children.text: the plan is funded exclusively by the purchase of individual insurance contracts,

children.label: B
children.text: such contracts provide for level annual premium payments to be paid extending not later than the retirement age for each individual participating in the plan, and commencing with the date the individual became a participant in the plan (or, in the case of an increase in benefits, commencing at the time such increase becomes effective),

children.label: C
children.text: benefits provided by the plan are equal to the benefits provided under each contract at normal retirement age under the plan and are guaranteed by an insurance carrier (licensed under the laws of a State to do business with the plan) to the extent premiums have been paid,

children.label: D
children.text: premiums payable for the plan year, and all prior plan years, under such contracts have been paid before lapse or there is reinstatement of the policy,

children.label: E
children.text: no rights under such contracts have been subject to a security interest at any time during the plan year, and

children.label: F
children.text: no policy loans are outstanding at any time during the plan year.

children.heading: Certain terminated multiemployer plans
children.label: 4 Certain terminated multiemployer plans
children.text: This section applies with respect to a terminated multiemployer plan to which section 4021 of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 applies until the last day of the plan year in which the plan terminates (within the meaning of section 4041A(a)(2) of such Act).
